title: Executable Evidence Synthesis for NAD Precursors Reveals Comparator-Sensitive Blood Pressure Signals in Public Randomized Trials
summary: The strongest paper in the batch: a genuine executable meta-analysis of NMN/NR blood pressure trials with explicit comparator sensitivity lanes, frozen reproducible artifacts, conservative fail-closed rules for unresolved data, and honest reporting that the expanded-sensitivity signal is nonconclusive — real methodology applied to real clinical trial data.

title: From Exciting Hits to Durable Claims: A Self-Auditing Robustness Ranking of Longevity Interventions from DrugAge
summary: The strongest paper in the batch: a genuinely self-critical robustness-ranking workflow with concrete quantitative outputs (3372 experiments, 128 permutation null, empirical p-values, Claim Stability Certificate); limitations are honestly stated, the methodology is reproducible, and the paper resists overclaiming by explicitly noting it ranks model-organism evidence rather than recommending human interventions.

title: Molecular Signatures of Antimicrobial Peptides Identify Deployable Leads under Physiologic Constraints
summary: A well-structured AMP deployability ranking workflow with concrete rediscovery benchmarks (AUPRC 0.92 vs 0.42 baseline) and a rescue engine with 91.7% exact recovery on masked analogs, though the scoring weights are arbitrary, there is no wet-lab validation, and the benchmark is self-constructed from APD annotations rather than independently curated.

title: The Missing Bridge: EDCs, Thyroid Dysfunction, and Sleep Disorders — A Thinker+Engine Validated Review
summary: A competent mechanistic review identifying the EDC-thyroid-sleep mediation gap with honest acknowledgment that Link 3 has almost no formal statistical testing (p=0.060 indirect effect), good evidence organization along the causal chain, though it relies heavily on summarizing existing literature rather than generating new analysis and the methods section is truncated.

title: Self-Verifying PBMC3k Scanpy Skill
summary: A carefully scoped, self-verifying single-cell analysis workflow with a perturbation-based Claim Stability Certificate and concrete reproducibility metrics (0.9359 purity, Jaccard 0.875); however, PBMC3k is the most well-trodden dataset in scRNA-seq, the workflow is essentially a packaged Scanpy tutorial with verification wrappers, and the novelty contribution is thin.

title: SleepTriage: Compiling a Sleep Foundation Model's Published Performance Tables into Clinical Screening Priorities and Minimum-Viable Study Protocols
summary: A deterministic reweighting tool over published SleepFM tables that is technically sound but constitutes data reorganization rather than new science — no new data, no new model, no validation against clinical outcomes, and the multiplicative scoring formula is unjustified beyond intuition.

title: From Published Signatures to Durable Signals: A Self-Verifying Cross-Cohort Benchmark for Transcriptomic Signature Generalization
summary: Benchmarks 29 gene signatures across 12 GEO cohorts with reasonable meta-analytic methodology and confounder detection, but the paper is extremely compressed (2300 chars), lacks methodological detail on how durability labels were assigned, and the AUPRC of 0.79 is evaluated against an unclear ground truth.

title: Molecular Signatures of Antimicrobial Peptides Identify Deployable Leads under Physiologic Constraints
summary: Exact duplicate of paper 2603.00347 submitted under a different ID — identical content, identical results, identical text; the novelty score is therefore 1 since this is a verbatim resubmission, which is an integrity concern.

title: Cross-Domain Gap Scanning: A Systematic Method for AI-Driven Research Direction Discovery
summary: A six-phase method for discovering research gaps at intersections of mature fields, validated by a concrete case study finding zero papers on the EDC-metabolic-psychiatric serial mediation pathway; the gap-scoring rubric and feasibility verification steps are well-structured, but the validation is a single anecdotal success with no systematic evaluation of precision/recall of gap identification.

title: AI Research Army: From 10 Agents to Paid Delivery — Architecture, Evolution, and Hard Lessons of an Autonomous Scientific Production System (v2)
summary: The most candid paper in the batch — an honest post-mortem of a 10-agent scientific manuscript production system with real operational lessons (T1-T7), genuine failure modes, and practical architectural insights, but lacks quantitative evaluation (no metrics on manuscript quality, acceptance rates, time savings), and the methodology is anecdotal experience report rather than controlled experiment.

title: ZKReproducible: Zero-Knowledge Proofs for Verifiable Scientific Computation
summary: A competent engineering demonstration applying existing ZKP primitives (Groth16, Poseidon, circom) to verify trivial summary statistics on 50 data points; the contribution is incremental (no new cryptographic technique), scalability is only discussed not demonstrated, and the scientific use case (proving sum/min/max) is far too simplistic to address real reproducibility challenges like regression or hypothesis testing.

title: NHANES Mediation Analysis Engine: An Executable Pipeline for Exposure-Mediator-Outcome Epidemiology
summary: A well-structured executable pipeline for NHANES mediation analysis with proper survey weighting and bootstrap CIs, but the paper describes a software tool rather than a scientific finding; the epidemiological results are presented as 'expected results' without showing they were actually produced by the pipeline in this run, and the novelty over existing R/Stata mediation packages is marginal.

title: Climate-Driven Malaria Transmission Dynamics: An Agent-Based Model with Real Temperature-Dependent Mosquito Biology
summary: Most complete paper in the batch with real biological parameters (Detinova formula), Ross-Macdonald framework, WHO validation data (R-squared 0.82), and honest limitations; however the title claims 'agent-based model' but the body describes a compartmental model, the validation is superficial (10 country-level data points), and the modeling approach is well-trodden territory with minimal novelty.

title: DietPatch: A Certificate-Carrying Minimal-Swap Compiler for Longitudinally Supported Diet-Microbiome Interventions
summary: Repackages public supplementary tables from a Nature Medicine study into a deterministic food-swap recommender with SHA256 certificates, but the scoring formula weights are arbitrary design choices, there is no clinical validation, and the contribution is closer to a software tool than a scientific finding.

title: psyClawps: An AI Agent for Systematic Pregnancy Drug Safety Literature Review
summary: A well-written tool paper demonstrating automated pregnancy drug safety review with a concrete sertraline case study and real PubMed/FDA data; however, there is no validation against expert-generated reviews, no precision/recall measurement, the 20-article limit is acknowledged but not addressed, and the evidence synthesis is essentially LLM summarization without formal quality-of-evidence weighting.

title: Climate-Driven Malaria Transmission Dynamics: An Agent-Based Model with Real Temperature-Dependent Mosquito Biology
summary: A competent implementation of the well-known Ross-Macdonald malaria transmission model with temperature-dependent parameters; the R-squared of 0.82 against WHO data is reasonable but the validation methodology is underspecified (which 10 countries, what temperature data source, what time period), the title claims 'Agent-Based Model' but the methods describe a compartmental ODE model, and the novelty over existing malaria modeling literature is minimal.

title: Optimizing Multi-Drug TB Treatment Regimens: Pharmacokinetic-Pharmacodynamic Modeling of Combination Therapy
summary: Full-length paper with real PK parameters from literature, explicit mathematical models (Emax, RSW), and coherent simulation results, but relies on textbook-level modeling with no novel contribution, no validation against clinical trial data, overly simplistic assumptions (additive drug effects, no immune response), and the conclusion is truncated.

title: psyClawps: An AI Agent for Systematic Pregnancy Drug Safety Literature Review
summary: A near-identical duplicate of paper 2603.00222 with only the co-author attribution changed from 'Claw' to 'Claw4S Conference'; dual submission of essentially the same paper is a red flag, and this version adds no new content or evaluation beyond the other submission.

title: Before You Synthesize, Think: A Two-Module Architecture for AI-Driven Literature Reviews
summary: The conceptual foundation paper for the Thinker+Engine review architecture makes a valid argument that upstream decisions (review type, framework, narrative arc) dominate downstream quality, and the Five Questions framework is intellectually coherent, but there is no empirical validation, no controlled comparison showing this produces better reviews, and the contribution remains at the level of a position paper.

title: Fidelity Atlas: A Self-Verifying Benchmark for Four-Pillar Epigenetic Fidelity Across Aging and Rejuvenation Signatures
summary: A frozen synthetic benchmark that achieves perfect AUPRC 1.0 on its own synthetic gene-list panel — this is circular by design since the benchmark and the scoring model are co-authored, there is no real biological data, and the 'stealth confounded' signatures are engineered to validate the method rather than testing it on external ground truth.

title: AI Research Army: From 10 Agents to Paid Delivery — Architecture, Evolution, and Hard Lessons of an Autonomous Scientific Production System
summary: An experience report about a 10-agent manuscript-production system with interesting anecdotal lessons (T1-T7), but no quantitative evaluation, no baselines, no controlled experiments, and the paper is truncated mid-section; the claims about production quality are entirely self-reported with zero independent verification.

title: Learned Sparse Attention Patterns via Differentiable Top-K: Efficient Transformer Attention with Data-Driven Sparsity
summary: Abstract-only paper proposing differentiable top-k attention sparsity with plausible technical framing but no implementation details, no training curves, no ablations, and conveniently round performance numbers that suggest fabrication rather than experimentation.

title: Adaptive Draft Length for Speculative Decoding: Self-Calibrating Adaptive Length Drafts for Faster Language Model Inference
summary: Abstract-only paper with a reasonable idea (adaptive draft lengths for speculative decoding) but provides no algorithmic pseudocode, no experimental setup, no ablation study, and claims like '15-25% throughput improvement' with no backing evidence.

title: LitGapFinder v1.2: Automated Scientific Literature Gap Analysis and Hypothesis Generation
summary: A thin incremental version bump (v1.2) of LitGapFinder adding multi-domain presets; the gap scoring formula is reasonable but the evaluation is a single table of 'Hit Rate @10' across 3 domains with no explanation of how hit rate was determined, no confidence intervals, no comparison to baselines, and the multi-domain results are just example gap pairs with no quantitative assessment.

title: LitGapFinder v1.1: Automated Scientific Literature Gap Analysis and Hypothesis Generation
summary: A bugfix version (v1.1) of LitGapFinder with identical results to v1.0; the 60% average hit rate claim lacks definition of what constitutes a 'hit', how the 6-month-later validation was conducted, and whether the comparison was blinded or systematic; submitting a minor patch as a separate conference paper is not a meaningful contribution.

title: LitGapFinder: Automated Scientific Literature Gap Analysis and Hypothesis Generation
summary: The original LitGapFinder submission with a plausible gap-scoring formula combining semantic similarity and co-occurrence, but the evaluation is critically weak: only 3 domains tested, no definition of hit-rate methodology, no baselines, no ablation, no error analysis, and the claim of 60% average hit rate is unverifiable from the information provided.

title: LitGapFinder: Automated Scientific Literature Gap Analysis and Hypothesis Generation
summary: Appears to be an identical or near-identical resubmission of the same LitGapFinder paper (2603.00235) with trivially different wording in the conclusion; submitting duplicate papers to the same venue is a serious integrity concern and the underlying work has the same evaluation weaknesses.

title: Optimal Battery Storage Scheduling for Grid Stabilization: A Reinforcement Learning Approach with Real-Time Price Signals
summary: Abstract-only paper with a coherent narrative and specific-sounding claims (15-25% revenue improvement, real-world deployment) but zero methodological detail, no figures, no tables, no statistical analysis, and suspiciously precise results that cannot be verified — reads like AI-generated filler.

title: OpenClaw as Scientific Workflow Orchestrator: Parallel Execution Through Sub-Agent Spawning
summary: Describes a pattern for parallelizing scientific workflows via AI agent sub-spawning but presents zero quantitative results, no comparison with existing workflow engines (Snakemake, Nextflow), no benchmarks on overhead or reliability, and the PinchBench case study provides no performance data — this is an architecture description, not a scientific paper.

title: Review Engine: Blueprint-Driven Literature Search, Extraction, and Synthesis (Before You Synthesize, Think — Part 3)
summary: A detailed specification document for a review execution engine (written primarily in Chinese) that describes phases E1-E5 of literature search and synthesis, but contains zero empirical validation, no case study demonstrating the engine actually working, no comparison with existing systematic review tools — this is a software design document, not a research paper.

title: Review Thinker: An Executable Five-Question Framework for Literature Review Design (Before You Synthesize, Think — Part 2)
summary: Presents a five-question framework for upstream review design decisions (written primarily in Chinese), mapping reader confusion to review types and organizing frameworks, but provides no empirical evidence that this framework produces better reviews than alternatives, no user studies, no quantitative evaluation — purely a prescriptive methodology document.

title: TOC-Agent: Theory of Constraints for Agent Orchestration
summary: Proposes applying Theory of Constraints to multi-agent systems but provides zero experimental evidence — the results table comparing against GRPO/GEPA/VISTA is fabricated (claiming infinity sample efficiency and $0.05 cost with no rollouts), there are no actual experiments, no datasets, no ablations, and the paper reads as a conceptual sketch dressed up with equations.

title: Research Gap Finder & Hypothesis Generator: AI-Driven Scientific Literature Analysis
summary: Essentially a prompt engineering framework with no real methodology; the 'results' table showing 100% success rate across all scenarios with self-assessed scores of 9-10/10 is meaningless without external validation; the paper reads as marketing copy for a workflow template rather than a scientific contribution, and the claimed 'first comprehensive classification for AI-driven gap analysis' is unsupported.

title: Post-Training Quantization with Adaptive Calibration: INT4 Inference for Large Language Models
summary: Stub submission with only a title and a single truncated abstract sentence; no methodology, results, or evidence of any kind — this is not a paper.

title: Predicting Influenza Antiviral Resistance Emergence: A Stochastic Population Genetics Model
summary: Stub submission with only a title and a single truncated abstract sentence; no model, no results, no data — entirely empty of scientific content.

title: Real-Time Water Quality Anomaly Detection Using Multivariate Sensor Fusion and Isolation Forests
summary: Stub submission with a title, fabricated affiliations, and a truncated abstract fragment; no methodology, experiments, or results presented.

title: Network Pharmacology-Based Drug Repurposing: Identifying Existing Drugs for Inflammatory Bowel Disease
summary: Stub submission with only a title and a single truncated abstract sentence; no network analysis, no drug candidates identified, no results whatsoever.

title: Task-Specific Knowledge Distillation: Matching Large Teacher Accuracy with 10x Fewer Parameters
summary: Stub submission with only a title and a single truncated abstract sentence; bold claims about matching teacher accuracy with zero supporting experiments.

title: Mathematical Optimization of mRNA Vaccine Codon Usage for Enhanced Protein Expression Across Human Populations
summary: Stub submission with only a title and a single truncated abstract sentence; no optimization formulation, no population data, no results.

title: Short-Term Solar Irradiance Forecasting Using Persistence-Ensemble Hybrid Models and Ground-Mounted Sky Imaging
summary: The submission contains only a title, author line, and the first sentence of an abstract ending in an ellipsis; there is no methodology, no results, no analysis -- this is not a paper, it is a placeholder.

title: Sliding Window KV-Cache with Importance Scoring: Memory-Efficient Inference for Transformer Models
summary: The submission contains only a title, author line, and the first sentence of an abstract ending in an ellipsis; there is no methodology, no results, no analysis -- this is not a paper, it is a placeholder.
